description: Movement/pathing audit (ED-1096) fix-plan EXECUTED end-to-end (steps 1-7 + decision gates 2/4) plus a 5-dimension adversarial-review pass, on the mass-battle lane's designated branch claude/mass-battle-audit-5c6nih. First allocation on this lane under the ED-<LANE>-NNNN namespace (ED-IN-0001) -- prior in-code/in-doc citations of 'ED-1097' throughout this work (units.py, config.py, bat.py, engine.py, gauge_mb.py, equipment/__init__.py, troop_types/registry.py, test_mass_battle_maneuvers.py, coverage_matrix.md) predate this branch learning of the cutover and are RENUMBERED to this ID; ED-1095/1096 (filed earlier this session, before the cutover) are unaffected and remain valid flat IDs (see id_reservations.yaml's verified_live_max note). ED-1096 established the root cause: since the coordinate field became default (ED-1089), 'envelop'/'sweep'/wheel maneuver instructions only ever worked on the legacy grid path (advance_cells) -- the live default node path (_node_advance) was a pure straight-line centroid attractor that ignored them entirely. FIX-PLAN STEPS EXECUTED: (1) Subunit._rekey_node_state fixes check_drift's node-state corruption on a mid-battle shape change. (2) orchestration.reset_positions made a deliberate no-op for node-path atoms (position genuinely does not reset between engagement turns within a battle, per Jordan). Decision gate 2 (weapon-derived unit_type): troop_types.registry.unit_type_for derives unit_type from the troop's assigned weapon (equipment.TROOP_LOADOUT/loadout_for), wired into build_unit/build_army; kite's steering gate decoupled from unit_type=='ranged' (kite is weapon-independent per Jordan, best with a bow but executable by lance cavalry -- only volley fire itself still requires ranged). (4) Restored the v12 lateral file-holding term in _node_advance (a sibling in a multi-subunit Unit holds its own deployment file instead of collapsing onto the shared centroid column). (5) Fixed the node WHEEL's 180-degree facing stall (lerp-normalize replaced with a rotation-based update). (6) Re-pointed the maneuver acceptance validators (validators.py's v_envelop/v_sweep) at the node path via a new path parameter and _set_movement_path, landed as tests/valoria/test_mass_battle_maneuvers.py. (7) Added the waypoint primitive -- Subunit._resolve_maneuver_goal/_envelop_goal/_sweep_goal -- giving _node_advance a per-tick, live-geometry-derived anchor goal for 'envelop'/'sweep', modeled on the legacy per-cell two-state machine at anchor granularity. Decision gate 4 (PER_CELL default flip): config.py's PER_CELL default '0'->'1' (same ED-1089 precedent), unlocking charge shock/ brace recoil/cavalry speed/fatigue/the ED-1091/ED-1095 gates by default; found and fixed two latent 'independently re-derive PER_CELL from a stale hardcoded os.environ default' bugs in bat.py and gauge_mb.py that would have silently mislabeled/misgated a bare invocation the moment the config default changed. DECISION GATES 1 AND 3 EXPLICITLY DEFERRED (Jordan: sequencing ruling, gates 1/3 occur AFTER pathing/routing is confirmed working) -- Command/Discipline-gated conditional tactics and the facing/attention split are NOT built here, by design. ADVERSARIAL REVIEW (5-dimension Workflow, sonnet finders + opus verify, per Jordan's 'proceed that way re workflow' instruction): 8 findings, 6 CONFIRMED + 1 PLAUSIBLE fixed same session (kite steering never ported to the node path -- a mounted_archers subunit correctly got instructions=('kite',...) from gate 2 but fell through to plain centroid steering and closed to melee, the exact bug class this whole audit exists to fix, for a 4th instruction the first pass missed, closed via a new _kite_goal; an escort atom's column-tracking was silently overridden by the sibling-column-holding fallback the instant its escorted unit's column diverged, defeating Stage C's own escort machinery; a test fixture leak; a dead constructor parameter converted to an assertion; a unit_type=None sentinel inconsistency between build_unit/build_army; two documentation-provenance corrections), 1 REFUTED (a real but non-defect test-coverage gap, flagged not fixed). VERIFICATION: all 4 bat.py digest modes (unit/cell grid, unit_field/cell_field node) byte-exact throughout every step against their respective baselines (2 unchanged grid, 2 deliberately re-recorded field -- isolated via bisection to steps 1/4/5/7, not a regression); full tests/valoria suite green (88 passed/10 skipped/1 xfailed after this branch merged origin/main's ED-IN-0001/0002 infrastructure). Functional: a build_envelopment wing tracked tick-by-tick via workbench/trace.py under DEFAULT toggles wheeled from its starting flank (row 36) to row 19 -- past the defender's own settled row (24.6) -- while holding its own flank column, a genuine double-envelopment geometry, not a straight walk-in; delivered to Jordan as an interactive tick-by-tick Artifact re-tracing the exact H4 (Envelopment vs Arrowhead) scenario shown broken earlier in the engagement. gauge_mb.py re-run under PER_CELL=1: single=2/20, multi=4/20. DISCLOSED, NOT CHASED (per this repo's standing discipline against retuning magnitudes to fit a band): a combat-PACING interaction where PER_CELL's newly-live fatigue/attrition/envelopment-sigma mechanics resolve a frontal engagement faster than a wide detour can complete -- confirmed non-movement via direct isolation (forcing orchestration.PER_CELL=False alone restores test_envelop_reaches_rear_node's pass; an isolated single-subunit fight at the same troop ratio favors the attacker 14-0-6/20 seeds; varying travel distance barely moved the outcome) and visible at battle-composition scale in the gauge re-run (H3/H4(Cannae)/H5/H6 lose 0-13% instead of the expected 45-72% band, consistent with an envelopment composition's pinning center routing before its wings complete the maneuver). Landed as a loud, documented xfail(strict=False) with the full diagnostic chain in its own docstring; the underlying combat-balance/pacing question (numerically-dominant vs. thin-and-yielding pinning force; a maneuver time-budget separate from the frontal fight's own clock) is flagged for whoever next works PER_CELL=1 combat balance -- out of this movement/pathing fix's scope. Full detail: tests/coverage_matrix.md's 2026-07-02 movement/pathing-audit entries; designs/audit/2026-07-02-mass-battle-movement-pathing-audit/README.md.
source: tests/sim/mass_battle/hierarchy/units.py, orchestration.py, validators.py, config.py, engine.py, troop_types/registry.py, equipment/__init__.py, bat.py, gauge_mb.py; tests/valoria/test_mass_battle_maneuvers.py; tests/coverage_matrix.md

description: Mass-battle Cannae gauge-band root-cause audit RATIFIED (merged via PR #73, ED-1094 merge-ratifies-by-default). Follow-up to ED-MB-0001's disclosed-not-chased finding that PER_CELL's combat mechanics collapse the H3-H6 Cannae-pattern historical gauge rows from a passing baseline to 0-13% losses against a 45-72% band. Produced by a Fable-led Workflow (4 parallel research agents; 4 independent adversarial diagnostic lenses -- clock_coupling, historical_fidelity, alternative_mechanism, design_options; each cross-critiqued by an independent Fable pass; Fable reconciliation re-verifying every contested fact against the working tree before adjudicating; a final Sonnet pass authoring the fix-plan document). RANKED ROOT CAUSE: demotes this session's own prior 'two racing clocks' hypothesis (test_envelop_reaches_rear_node's docstring) -- refuted as a general/gauge-scale account by gauge row C7 (a genuinely multi-subunit cavalry envelopment) passing its band, though it survives narrowly at the original diagnostic fixture's own scope. RC-1 (VERY HIGH confidence): three composition-coupling defects in the pool/morale accounting layer -- (a) a composed subunit's combat pool scales down per simultaneous-engagement pair while a single-subunit opponent rolls full pool into every pair (orchestration.py:504-528, POOL_VARIANT=C-ii); (b) two subunits' independent morale triggers double-erode one shared parent-Unit morale pool because build_army never populates a subunit's own morale field (hierarchy/units.py erode_morale/cohesion ~L404-431); (c) PER_CELL's casualty-fraction triggers use subunit-scale denominators (core/state.py:27-38). Plus two independent narrower bugs (a validator fixture ghost-cell construction-order bug, validators.py:205; a float-epsilon pool-floor bug, orchestration.py:527-528). RC-2 (HIGH confidence): the old PER_CELL=0 baseline that used to pass H3-H6 was itself an invincibility-bug artifact (multi-subunit cohesion structurally frozen at 1.0), not a working model to restore. RC-3 (MEDIUM, confounded): RC-1's couplings plausibly extend to gauge scale but this is not proven -- C4-vs-C7 (identical _envelop_army composition, one fails its band, one passes) is the sharpest unexplained datapoint. RC-4 (design gap, not a defect): the engine has no state between eroding and routed, so Hannibal's actual thin-and-yielding center tactic is currently inexpressible -- correctly deferred until RC-1 is fixed. RC-5 (LOW-MEDIUM, undiagnosed): 9 of 20 gauge rows fail for reasons unrelated to composition coupling at all -- flagged as a separate, not-yet-opened lane item. FIX PLAN (deliberately narrow, gate-free work only): 2 independent bug fixes safe to build now, 3 measurement/ablation steps (a per-coupling ablation harness; a gauge-scale frozen-wings ablation; a C4-vs-C7 mechanism trace) to close the remaining open questions -- explicitly does not build the accounting fix itself. DECISION GATES DG-3 and DG-4 RULED THE SAME DAY (recorded as an addendum in the audit doc, not re-litigated here): DG-3 = Option A, split the defender's pool symmetrically across pairs (Jordan's stated criterion: long-term integrity and fidelity to reality -- Option A reuses the existing pool-split mechanism symmetrically rather than inventing an underived engage-fraction formula (B) or a band-fit magnitude bump (C), and is the physically correct model besides). DG-4 = 'a blend of per-subunit as well as whole unit' morale (Jordan, verbatim) -- operationalized by wiring ALREADY-EXISTING, currently-inert machinery rather than authoring new state: hierarchy/units.py's agg_morale()/derive_rout() (~L1460-1481, troop-weighted aggregate for whole-unit rout) and cascade_morale_hit() (~L1482-1491, army-wide contagion) already implement the blend; the only gap is that build_army/build_envelopment/build_refused_flank never populate a subunit's own morale field at construction (confirmed by direct read, engine.py:243-246), so every subunit falls through to the shared parent pool. The fix is to default each subunit to its own real starting morale value at construction, closing the double-count by construction with zero new state. DG-1, DG-2, DG-5 remain OPEN, gated on the DG-3/DG-4 fix landing and the gauge being re-measured. Implementation of the fix plan + DG-3/DG-4 rulings NOT yet started as of this entry -- this ED records the audit's ratification and the two same-day gate rulings only.

Follow-up to ED-MB-0002: DG-3/DG-4 landed (PR #75) but did NOT close the H3-H6 Cannae-pattern gauge-band gap (draws unchanged). A fresh Fable-5-led adversarial audit of the shipped DG-3/DG-4 code found four concrete, reproducible engine defects surviving that fix, two introduced/made load-bearing by the DG-3 rewrite itself: D1, an outer troops_frac/army-size divisor applied to a subunit base_pool BEFORE pair_pool_contribution's own per-troop normalization, diluting a composed subunit's pool by its share of the WHOLE ARMY's troops rather than its own troop type/quality/density (orchestration.py, POOL_VARIANT=="C-ii"); D2, _envelop_goal's phase-1/phase-2 transition sharing one threshold for entry and exit with no hysteresis, producing a permanent limit cycle where a wheeling wing never actually reaches contact (hierarchy/units.py); D3, routed/broken atoms resurrected to combat pool 1 by an unconditional max(1,...) floor, contradicting SS A.12 "cannot fight back" (orchestration.py); D4, distribute_casualties tracking engaged columns as one union across a whole Unit, letting an uninvolved subunit (e.g. a far wing) absorb a different subunit's casualties by column coincidence (percell.py). Plus a harness-level force-ratio bug (gauge_mb.py's _envelop_army/_refused_army fielded 3x/2x a single-subunit opponent's troops, a silent side-effect of the LC-8 migration, making every DG-1 composition question untestable). JORDAN RULINGS (2026-07-05, via AskUserQuestion): DG-3 completion = "Intensive (per-troop, partition-invariant)" pool semantics -- the outer troops_frac multiplier removed entirely from the C-ii branch (pair_pool_contribution already normalizes per-troop internally). DG-1 = "symmetric at parity + majority pin cavalry wing so long as bottom-up emergent primitives approach" -- _envelop_army/_refused_army rebuilt at force parity (same total troops as their single-subunit opponent) via the continuous-scale troops/concentration path; infantry rows (H3/H5/H6) keep the symmetric center+2-wings shape at parity; cavalry rows (C4/C7) rebuilt as a majority (2/3) infantry pin + minority cavalry envelopment wings, matching the Polybius/Livy order of battle, via engine.build_envelopment unmodified. DG-2 (fighting-withdrawal/yield mechanic) = "create as workplan" -- NOT implemented this pass; captured as designs/proposals/mass_battle_fighting_withdrawal_v1.md, status PROPOSED. All four D1-D4 fixes + both ratified decisions implemented; an independent adversarial review found two of the six changes had real defects (D3's pool-zeroing was a no-op -- roll_pool/_sigma_net_boost both re-floor pool to a minimum of 1 internally regardless of the zeroed input, so the actual fix forces a_net/b_net to exactly 0 directly; gauge_mb.py's new wing_speed/speed kwargs never reached Unit.speed since Subunit has no speed field and it was never forwarded to build_envelopment/build_refused_flank) -- both fixed and re-verified. All 4 bat.py digest modes re-recorded (this touches shared, non-gated combat-resolution code, as ED-MB-0002's own fix did). tests/valoria 88 passed/16 skipped(numpy,unrelated)/1 xfailed. HONEST RESULT: draws are now GONE across H3/H4/H5/H6/C4 (were 100%/90%/100%/100%/66.7%), a genuine and dramatic change -- but these rows now OVERSHOOT their historical bands decisively in the attacker's favor (WIN-OUT) instead of landing inside them, except C7 which lands cleanly at the top of its wide band. Full 20-row gauge aggregate moved only marginally (4/20 -> 5/20 passing, multi mode n=30 -- H1,C1,C2,C6,C7; C1 newly passes). A controlled, isolated experiment (co-located vs spatially-separated equal-troop subunit splits, both vs an identical single-subunit opponent) suggests why: subunit_combat_pool's Command-driven combat score is not scaled by a subunit's own share of army-wide troops, so multiple spatially-separated attacking fronts against one defender can each roll close to a full, independent combat score simultaneously, tempered only by a small ENCIRCLEMENT_PENALTY tax that in this configuration falls on the defender, not the attacker. Whether this is a genuine partition-invariance defect needing its own fix, or the historically-correct emergent mechanism for why real encirclements are so devastating (with the bands themselves needing reconsideration), is explicitly NOT decided here -- flagged as a new, unresolved architecture question requiring its own dedicated Jordan ruling, not a silent magnitude tweak. RC-5's 9 unrelated single-subunit rows remain a separate, not-yet-opened lane item; DG-5 (racing-clocks) is corrected/re-confirmed closed (the original ablation's null result was itself confounded by the now-fixed D2 bug, not evidence of a genuinely settled non-race).

OPT-13, filed per Jordan's 2026-07-07 "ratify all" ruling (ED-IN-0026). Gauge triage continuation: the C-EMERGE full run (multi 6/20; overshoot measured on plain formations H2/H7/H9 and reverse rows H10/H11; R3 unresolvable; single-mode 2/20 mass-UNRESOLVED) -> per-row triage feeding the RC-5/DG-1 rulings. Data: 01_workings/emergence/gauge_mb_full_run_2026-07-07.txt. RESOLVED 2026-07-08: Jordan ruled (AskUserQuestion) the ED-MB-0003 partition-invariance open question = "genuine defect -- fix it". Fixed: subunit_combat_pool's Command-driven score is a per-atom score, not a per-troop rate; pair_pool_contribution correctly renormalizes ONE atom split across MULTIPLE enemies but did nothing when SEVERAL atoms of one side converge on the SAME single opposing atom (a pinning center + 2 wings all hitting one Line/Arrowhead defender -- H3-H6/C4/C7's exact shape), so each converging atom contributed its own near-full base_pool, multiplying total dice by the convergence-group size for identical total troops. New core/exchange.py:_pair_engaged_troops + orchestration.py:_convergence_scale/PC_CONVERGENCE_NORM (default ON) renormalize any such group to what one merged atom of the combined troops would contribute; a group of size 1 (every single-subunit row) is an exact no-op. Verified live (direct trace, not inferred): fires on 1446/1686 sampled ticks of an H3-style battle, max group size 3. All 4 bat.py digests re-recorded (shared, non-gated code, same discipline as every prior DG-3/DG-4/Step-4 landing). tests/valoria 112 passed/57 skipped/1 xfailed/0 failed (7 pre-existing test_names.py failures confirmed unrelated via git-stash bisection). HONEST RESULT: gauge re-run (multi, n=60) shows the fix does NOT move H3/H4/H5/H6/C4's win/loss/draw split at all (bit-for-bit identical decA/dec_n) even though per-trial hp/turn values changed -- the defect was real and is now closed, but was never the dominant lever for these rows' overshoot (envelopment/charge-shock morale collapse dominates). Full 20-row gauge unchanged (single 2/20, multi 6/20). RC-5 preliminary finding (diagnostic only, not solved): a controlled A/B-slot-swap experiment on 3 RC-5 pairs (Arrowhead/Line, GappedLine/Line, GappedLine/Arrowhead) found an inconsistent slot-dependent asymmetry not explained by a uniform side-bias or shape hierarchy -- a true Line-Line mirror stays near-even (17/13 of 30), ruling out a blanket engine-wide side bug, while the three asymmetric pairs each flip direction differently. Likely shared ingredient (not traced further): ANCHOR_MAP's per-shape deployment column (Line=9/Arrowhead=8/GappedLine=7) is applied regardless of which side carries that shape, so differently-shaped sides deploy at different absolute columns. Flagged as the next concrete lead, not claimed solved; RC-5's other 6 rows (H7,H8,R1,R3,C1,C3,C5) not investigated this pass. Jordan also ruled DG-2 = "build it now" (see its own workplan doc / handoff for build status). Full record: tests/coverage_matrix.md 2026-07-08 entry.

DG-2 (fighting-withdrawal/yield mechanic), Jordan-ruled 2026-07-08 "build it now" (designs/proposals/mass_battle_fighting_withdrawal_v1.md). Built exactly the proposal doc's own §4 step 1 ("lowest-risk slice"): state + commanded-entry only, NOT emergent auto-entry (§2.2's second bullet, explicitly deferred pending its own measurement), NOT rally/pocket exits (§2.4, beyond the free rout-collapse exit which needed no new code). New Subunit.yielding: bool = False (default-inert) + a yield_active property (the single shared gate every consumption site reads: yielding and eff_discipline >= D_YIELD and unit_type != 'ranged' -- discipline-gated + melee-only per §2.5). 'yielding' added to _ORDER_SAFE_FIELDS so a 'yield' order composes with the existing Stage-C Order/check_orders machinery with zero new order-primitive code. Movement: new Subunit._yield_goal (reuses _kite_goal's reflect-through-anchor flee vector, always active) wired into _resolve_maneuver_goal behind yield_active; step capped at 1 cell/tick. Node/field path only (same scope boundary 'envelop'/'sweep' already have; the legacy grid advance_cells path has its own separate inline dispatch, untouched this pass). Facing: a bespoke yield_active-gated override at both cell_facing_vec write sites, firing regardless of PC_FACING_MODEL (which defaults OFF) -- locks facing toward target_atom's centroid even while the anchor moves away, the doc's "mechanically load-bearing" distinction from rout. Combat pool: subunit_combat_pool multiplies by YIELD_POOL_MULT when yield_active -- reduced but never zero. Anti-abuse: orchestration.py's volley fire() refuses to fire for a yield_active atom (redundant with melee-only, kept for defence-in-depth). Both new magnitudes explicitly flagged [CALIBRATED-DEBT] per the doc's own §5 (not independently derived): D_YIELD=3 reuses this file's own disc_mult tier break; YIELD_POOL_MULT reuses PC_SHOCK_HOLD_BRACE (0.35) verbatim. VERIFICATION: all 4 bat.py digests confirmed BYTE-IDENTICAL (no re-record -- yielding defaults False everywhere in the battery, genuinely inert not just claimed). New tests/valoria/test_mass_battle_yield.py (9 tests, all passing): default-inert, discipline gate, melee-only gate, _yield_goal's flee-vector math, order-safety, pool malus present/absent, and a real-battle integration test confirming the yielding attacker actually moves AND its facing vector keeps a non-negative dot product toward its target. Full tests/valoria suite green, no regressions. HONEST MEASUREMENT (§4 step 2's ask, n=20, node path, build_envelopment center+2-wings vs a single-subunit Line defender): center-yields-from-tick-0 vs no-yield -- center hp retained rises 35.8%->40.6% (the mechanism works as built), but the attacking army's win rate collapses 70%->0% (14/0/6 -> 0/19/1 wins/losses/draws) when the center yields UNCONDITIONALLY for the entire battle -- the crudest possible always-on policy trades far more offense than the center's own marginal survival gains recoup within this scenario. Not evidence the mechanic is broken; Cannae's historical yield was TIMED to buy exactly enough time for the wings, not sustained the whole battle -- a timed/conditional entry (an Order with a tick:N trigger, or an emergent entry keyed to encirclement progress) is the natural next experiment, not attempted this pass. Reported honestly per the doc's own instruction, not oversold. NOT built this pass (disclosed): emergent auto-entry, rally exit, pocket exit.

Jordan direct instruction (verbatim): "Please consider abandoning combat pools being related to the commander, and instead being solely derived from the subunit troop type, quality and numbers." Follow-up clarification: "And I guess subunit power is the aggregate or derivation of cell power." SUPERSEDES ED-899's Command-only combat-pool base (designs/provincial/mass_battle_v30.md SS A.1/A.4) -- Command remains live for morale, formation-hold speed, order-issuing, and derive_rout's Command-0 condition, just not the dice pool. New POOL_QUALITY_MODEL toggle (config.py, default ON): base pool = eff_power x eff_size x POOL_QUALITY_SCALE. eff_power is the troop-TYPE quality stat (TROOP_TYPE_STATS/SS B.2 -- levy=1 through knights_templar=5; SS A.1 itself already names Power as "determines dice rolled"). eff_size is NUMBERS (current troops / BLOCK_SIZE, continuously degrading with casualties -- no separate cohesion multiplier needed since eff_size itself is the smoothly-shrinking quantity). POOL_QUALITY_SCALE=0.5 [CALIBRATED] renormalizes the product to reproduce the historical T3-infantry baseline magnitude (~8, matching the old command=4/full-cohesion baseline) so pen/stam_pen's existing unscaled discipline/stamina penalties keep roughly their historical relative weight. Per Jordan's cell-power follow-up: eff_power x eff_size is already exactly the cell-level aggregate whenever a subunit's cells share one troop type (true today -- no per-cell troop_type exists yet, same gap pair_pool_contribution's own docstring already names); documented as such rather than adding a redundant per-cell loop here, since pair_pool_contribution/_pair_engaged_troops already do the real per-cell troop-density redistribution for pair-scoped resolution and will pick up true per-cell power the moment that data exists. Applied consistently to BOTH core/exchange.py:subunit_combat_pool (the live per-pair combat resolution) and hierarchy/units.py:Unit.base_combat_pool (the pursuit/rout path), which had been missed by a naive edit and would have left an inconsistency between main combat and pursuit mechanics. COMMAND_SIGMA_ENABLED's two prior branches remain live and selectable via POOL_QUALITY_MODEL=0, per this repo's standing ablation-toggle discipline -- not deleted, superseded-as-default. VERIFICATION: all 4 bat.py digests re-recorded (shared, non-gated combat-resolution code, same discipline as every prior pool-formula landing in this lane). tests/valoria: 121 passed/57 skipped/1 xpassed/7 failed (6 pre-existing test_names.py failures, unrelated; 1 expected digest-drift failure now fixed by the re-record). New xpass: test_envelop_reaches_rear_node unexpectedly passed once under the new model (not re-verified across seeds, xfail marker left in place per honest-disclosure discipline, not claimed as a reliable fix). HONEST GAUGE RESULT (multi, n=60): 6/20 -> 7/20 pass. Newly passing: C4 (cavalry envelopment, was WIN-OUT, now 83.3% inside 75-95 band), C5 (shaken-line exploit, now 95% inside 65-98). Newly failing/flipped: H4, the actual Cannae matchup, flips from attacker WIN-OUT (96.6%) to attacker LOSING badly (1.7% A / 65% B / 33.3% draws) -- a genuine, disclosed trade-off: single-large-subunit cavalry rows correctly reward raw numbers now, but composed-army rows lose because their PER-ATOM troop counts are individually smaller than the single consolidated defender's, an emergent property of the new formula, not a bug. OPEN, DISCLOSED RESIDUAL: lanchester_signature.py's strict trajectory-fit law-exponent check (melee should conserve p<=1.4, the linear law) fails under BOTH models. The PRE-EXISTING Command-driven baseline (POOL_QUALITY_MODEL=0, i.e. what was already in production before this session) already measures p~1.55 there -- a previously-undetected gap, confirmed unrelated to this change via git-stash bisection against the pre-session commit. That same baseline's simpler win-rate check also showed a 2:1 melee army losing 97% of the time; a quick trace suggests this specific check's methodology (a single 18-tick run_battle call, which mostly ends in a draw at this troop ratio) may be measuring decisive-outcome sampling noise rather than a structural defect -- flagged, NOT independently confirmed either way, left for whoever next touches this test. Under the new model, the simpler win-rate check now correctly PASSES (100% big-army win, +53.7 casualty differential -- the qualitative "bigger army should win" property is restored) but the stricter exponent check gets WORSE, not better (p~2.50). Tested extensively before accepting this residual: a sqrt(eff_size) variant barely moved it (p~2.35); a uniform pool-magnitude scale swept across {1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.2, 0.15, 0.1, 0.0625, 0.03} plateaus at p~1.65-1.7 below ~0.15 and never reaches <=1.4 at any point tested. Confirmed NOT a Lanchester double-count: disabling LANCHESTER_ENABLED entirely leaves the exponent completely unchanged at p=2.5. Root cause (measured, not just theorized): the amplification is internal to the pool -> net-successes -> compute_degree tier -> DAMAGE_BY_DEGREE pipeline -- larger absolute pools have proportionally lower variance, so which discrete degree tier (Partial/Success/Overwhelming) each side lands in becomes near-deterministic from the pool ratio alone, and that discrete tier assignment compounds the ratio rather than passing it through linearly. NOT silently patched with an arbitrary scale constant chosen to look closer to the target -- a uniform scale provably cannot fix a ratio-sensitive test, since it does not change the win/loss ratio, only the absolute magnitude. Flagged as a genuine, not-yet-closed follow-up in designs/provincial/mass_battle_v30.md's own SS A.1 note and here: fixing it for real likely needs compute_degree's threshold logic or the DAMAGE_BY_DEGREE/Lanchester-coefficient calibration revisited, not another pool-formula tweak. Full numeric record: tests/coverage_matrix.md's third 2026-07-08 entry.

Full 11-surface agonist-antagonist architecture investigation into why the 20-row historically-grounded gauge (tests/sim/gauge_mb.py) is stuck at 7/20 (multi mode), requested directly by Jordan ("perform an investigation... work from all directions and surfaces", later widened mid-session to explicitly include "movement, pathing, routes, strategies, tactics, stances, reach" and "ranged weaponry"). Two Workflow waves (7 + 4 surfaces; producer -> isolated adversarial critic -> opus-4.8-max synthesis per wave, then a third opus-4.8-max combination pass reconciling both) fielded 24 subagents across ~1288 tool calls. Full record, all findings, bucket table, and the DG-6..DG-16 decision-point text: designs/audit/2026-07-08-mass-battle-gauge-architecture-audit/README.md (+ 01_wave1_synthesis.md / 02_wave2_synthesis.md for the per-wave detail this README reconciles). HONEST RESULT: the 13 nominal failures decompose to ~9 genuinely-deep engine divergences (2 rows, C3/H9, are n=60 sampling noise; 2 more, R1/R3, are harness-construction failures). Two mechanisms dominate, both causally proven by reversal ablation: E1, a cell-count/density plumbing defect (geometry.py's two unreconciled cell-count generators -> a static, never-recomputed Unit.ncells -> the Lanchester density term) that reverses H3/H10 outright when patched; and D1, a super-linear resolution architecture measured at melee exponent p~3.2 under the live PER_CELL=1 path (worse than ED-MB-0006's disclosed p~2.50 -- that figure was measured under the wrong PER_CELL setting by lanchester_signature.py's own hardcoded default). These two are multiplicative, not competing, and NEITHER ALONE LANDS A ROW IN-BAND -- this investigation is diagnostic, not curative, and that is disclosed as a first-class finding, not softened. GRANULARITY DIRECTIVE VERDICT: the per-cell quality/type axis (veteran-front/levy-rear) is confirmed byte-inert for all 20 rows by four independent lines of evidence -- a legitimate future architecture, not a gauge fix. The per-cell COUNT axis is already the #1 defect (E1). Troop-grounded speed and the entire ranged/volley pool were never brought under ED-MB-0006's POOL_QUALITY_MODEL and remain ungrounded. NEW EMPIRICAL RESULT (not present in either wave in isolation, found by the wave-2 synthesis's own probe): the two cheapest-looking fixes for the ranged rows (R1: correct archer stats; R3: the kite instruction) are MUTUALLY INCOMPATIBLE -- a correctly-statted discipline-3 archer computes a live movement step of exactly zero (no fractional-velocity accumulator on the node path), so kite cannot rescue a unit physically unable to move. This promotes a bug every producer rated latent/invisible into a fix-blocking prerequisite and reverses an earlier reading that had listed a ranged-hold variant as a safe, contained fix -- it is not; it is gated on DG-10. TEN NEW ENGINE DEFECTS (E1-E10) and ONE HARNESS-ARTIFACT CLUSTER identified and bucketed, full detail + file:line citations in the audit README's SS3/Appendix. ELEVEN NEW JORDAN-RULING DECISION POINTS OPENED: DG-6 (the resolution-architecture calibration itself -- the deepest, highest-leverage call) through DG-16 (tactical/role-layer grounding); DG-7..DG-9 renumbered from an earlier combination draft's DG-A..DG-D after a genuine renumbering-collision defect was caught and corrected (the draft had reused DG-1..DG-5, which are ALREADY RULED per ED-MB-0002/0003/0005 -- see the audit README's Appendix crosswalk). NOT YET IMPLEMENTED: this ED closes the investigation itself (status resolved); the ~10-item safe-to-fix list (SS7a) and all eleven DG-6..DG-16 rulings remain open follow-up work, gated primarily on Jordan's ruling on DG-6. [CALIBRATED-DEBT] flagged in place, not resolved: the currently-passing cavalry rows (C4, C5, C7) may be passing partly on the same E1/D1 artifacts targeted for removal -- the pass count is not confirmed monotonic in fixes applied.

Two contradictory ranged/volley Damage-Reduction tables coexist in params/mass_combat.md, both live (neither marked superseded/struck). (1) 'Projectile weapons (mass combat only -- PP-104)' table (~L191-200) gives Heavy-armour DR of LP/arrows=7, HP/bolts=5, LBl/sling=6, HBl/siege=2. (2) 'Ranged DR Table (Volley Phase) -- PP-188' table (~L303-312) gives Heavy vs Piercing=3, vs Blunt=2 -- roughly 2x apart from table (1) for the same armour band and damage type (arrows/Piercing 7 vs 3; bolts/Piercing 5 vs 3). An importer or player cannot determine which table governs volley resolution. Proposed remediation: Jordan rules which table is canonical (or scopes each to a distinct phase/context) and marks the other superseded. One of three MB-lane provenance-integrity defects from this run (see ED-MB-0009, ED-MB-0010). Surfaced by the 2026-07-13 multi-agent audit (mechanic_audit finding G1).
source: designs/audit/2026-07-13-multi-agent-audit/ (report SS5 item 1 / mechanic_audit G1); verified against params/mass_combat.md ~L191-200 vs ~L303-312

description: The mass_battle module contract in references/module_contracts.yaml (L468) emits a fabricated Key type 'scene_outcome.battle_concluded' labeled 'substrate SS8.5 verbatim', alongside the correct 'scene.battle_concluded' (L469, 'registry-declared form'). Canon writes 'scene.battle_concluded'; 'scene_outcome' is only the emit FAMILY name, not a type_id -- the SS8.5-verbatim provenance claim is false. The inline comment already flags cross-doc naming-form drift '[OPEN -- Jordan]' but no ED was filed. module_contracts.yaml is regenerate-never-hand-edit, but the spurious edge is a contract-correctness defect: remove the 'scene_outcome.battle_concluded' emit (it is not a registry extension). One of three MB-lane provenance-integrity defects this run (see ED-MB-0008, ED-MB-0009). Surfaced by the 2026-07-13 multi-agent audit (module_adjudicator P1-A).

description: DG-10 (opened by ED-MB-0007) resolved: the field/node movement path froze every sub-Discipline-5 body. _node_advance (the path FIELD_MOVEMENT=1 routes to since ED-1089) computed the per-tick advance as step = max(0, math.floor(base*disc_mult) + stance_mod); with the universal base cell-speed 1 and disc_mult 0.7 (disc 3-4) / 0.4 (disc<3), floor(1*0.7)=0 -> a balanced disc<5 formation never advanced to contact, making every such battle a vacuous 0-casualty draw. Per the mass_battle_v30 B.2 troop table this is the COMMON case: levy/light_infantry/heavy_infantry/archers/crossbow/sling/artillery are all disc<5; only cavalry(5)/knights_templar(6) cleared the floor (aggressive stance +1 or cavalry x2 the only other escapes). The continuous-velocity accumulator meant to prevent this (units.py head comment 'advances at its TRUE average rate instead of flooring to 0') was wired only into the LEGACY grid method advance_cells and sat dead there, never reaching _node_advance. Jordan ruled in-session ('fields, not grids. no grids.' / 'if it's broken and not commensurate with system, disable' / 'what even is the point of the continuous velocity accumulator?'): the math.floor is the grid-snap the coordinate field exists to remove, and the accumulator is itself only a Bresenham workaround for integer positions. Fix: on the FIELD path step is now the real velocity (no floor, no accumulator) -- _node_pos/_node_anchor are already floats and the sole consumer moves the anchor by eff=min(step,mag) along a unit vector; a disc4 body advances 0.7 cells/tick. Whole-number velocities are kept int so disc>=5 rows stay byte-exact; only genuinely fractional (disc<5) velocities become float. The legacy GRID path (FIELD_MOVEMENT=0) keeps its integer floor untouched -> the CI byte-exact grid oracle (test_mass_battle_byte_exact.py, pins FIELD_MOVEMENT=0) still passes. Field gauge goldens (bat.py cell_field/unit_field, NOT CI-checked): mirror/ranged (Line-vs-Line) stay byte-identical; the 8 decisive rows change because a unit that degrades below disc-5 MID-BATTLE (combat/charge shock) used to freeze in place and now keeps moving at its true reduced rate (confirmed by trace: wedge seed 0, side B -> disc 3) -- goldens re-recorded. NOTE: this fixes MOVEMENT only; it shifts the 20-row Cannae balance gauge (frozen units now fight) which is the DG-6-gated calibration surface -- NOT a balance-calibration claim, no balance constant tuned. The SAME grid-floor freeze exists in the WIRED engine (systems/mass_battle/sim/units.py:advance_cells, the one resolve_mass_battle/faction_action call) and is disabled there too (Jordan: 'subunits aren't moving when they have previously' / 'if it's broken and not commensurate with system, disable'); that engine holds integer cell positions (contact = set-membership on int coords) so it can't carry a true sub-cell field velocity (unreconciled with the field engine, ED-IN-0074 D5) -- the fix CLAMPS to the grid's minimum quantum (advancing body takes >=1 whole cell instead of a frozen zero) rather than carrying a faithful sub-cell velocity -- disclosed cost (adversarial pass): this flattens the sub-disc-5 speed gradient (disc 1/2/3/4 all move 1 cell/tick here, vs the field engine's 0.4/0.4/0.7/0.7); representing the gradient on this integer-contact grid would need the rejected accumulator or float positions, so the field engine stays the faithful one and this is an unfreeze stopgap. Discipline>=5 (incl. the wired resolve_mass_battle default of 5) is byte-identical (round(1.0)==floor(1.0); an adversarial A/B incl. mid-battle degradation and volley-under-approach found no disc>=5 outcome that differs -- degradation lags contact when movement is moot). Guarded by tests/valoria/test_mass_battle_systems_movement.py (8 tests). Surfaced by the 2026-07-22 field-based stress test (audit/2026-07-22-mass-battle-stress-test/).
source: audit/2026-07-22-mass-battle-stress-test/ (field-based stress test); verified via per-row bat.py digest diff (old vs fix) + mid-battle discipline-degradation trace

description: Spatial-model v2 Stage B+C (the CIRCLE->OBB contact + collision upgrade on the FIELD path, per audit/2026-07-22-mass-battle-stress-test/spatial_model_v2_plan.md, Jordan-directed 'Euclidean motion, boxed footprint'). TWO parts. (1) ANALYTIC swept-SAT time-of-impact: the parked Stage B+C prototype used a per-cell-pair scan+bisection on obb_front_reach_overlap (~200k list-allocating SAT calls/battle, field path ~20-60x slower, stalled the producing agent). Replaced with a closed-form _swept_first_overlap_s (hierarchy/units.py): over one tick box axes+corner-offsets are constant (Euclidean motion translates only the centre), so each SAT axis is a linear-in-s overlap band -> an s-interval; intersect the <=4 axes' intervals, first-touch = left edge. O(4)/pair, zero iteration, ~15us/pair. Verified against the reference scan+bisection to ~1e-15 over 700 seeded fuzzed pairs (281 genuine touches, 0 mismatches). (2) COLLIDE-not-decelerate model CORRECTION (Jordan in-session: 'why are we decelerating to halt instead of just colliding at whatever speed?' / 'and wouldn't that break charging?'). The plan (and the parked prototype) made the TOI HALT surface EQUAL the contact surface -- both the reach-extended box. That DEADLOCKS: resolve_toi_and_commit parks a closing cell EXACTLY on the obb_front_reach_overlap *touch* boundary, where the strict-overlap contact predicate returns False, so contact never fires -> a permanent 0-casualty standoff at gap 2*(CELL_RADIUS+reach) (reproduced: head-on Line-vs-Line froze at gap 1.5 for the entire battle; clean main reaches contact). Root cause: reach was being treated as a wall to decelerate against rather than a weapon envelope reaching across a gap. FIX: the hard geometric stop is BODY vs BODY (unit squares, reach 0 -- the only thing that physically cannot interpenetrate); contact/engagement stays on the reach-extended box (Stage B, core/contact.py _find_contacts_standoff, unchanged). The body surface sits strictly inside the reach surface (reach>0 always: the P-DEC-1 table is 0.1/0.2/0.3, current flat REACH_SHORT=0.5), so contact fires as cells close through the reach band into body contact -- bodies collide, weapons reach across the closing gap. The stop is symmetric+reach-independent so the reach THROTTLE (_reach_throttle/_effective_reach) is retired from the stop path (both cells cap at the shared body-touch fraction; reach asymmetry -- a longer weapon engaging first -- is expressed by contact firing earlier for the longer-reach box, not a movement handicap). CHARGING preserved/RESTORED: _momentum_speed reads cell_last_speed = the PRE-cap intended step (the charge velocity), so charge shock reflects closing speed; under the old reach-stop, contact never fired so charge shock never triggered at all (verified: head-on now closes to gap 1.0 body-touch and exchanges casualties; a Fast cavalry charge reaches contact and deals more than it takes on the impact tick). Guarded by tests/valoria/test_obb_contact_toi.py (7 tests: contact-fires-at-OBB, head-on no-body-interpenetration+no-stall, cavalry-charge-reaches-contact, grid-path-never-calls-OBB, conservation I1, determinism I2) + test_obb_primitive.py (Stage A, 21 tests, unchanged). I4 GRID ORACLE UNTOUCHED: resolve_toi_and_commit runs only under 'if FIELD_MOVEMENT' (orchestration.py:1405); the FIELD_MOVEMENT=0 byte-exact grid oracle (test_mass_battle_byte_exact.py) is structurally unreachable and stays green. FIELD behaviour changes by design (bodies now close to touch and fight instead of standing off at gap 1.5 doing nothing) -> the bat.py field goldens (cell_field/unit_field, NOT CI-checked per test_mass_battle_byte_exact.py head comment) re-recorded. This MOVES the DG-6-gated 20-row Cannae balance gauge (units that used to freeze at range now engage) -- disclosed, NOT a balance-calibration claim, no balance constant tuned (I5). Plan Stage C + risk R1 amended in spatial_model_v2_plan.md to record the body-stop correction. Departs from the written plan's shared-surface premise -- flagged prominently in the PR body for merge-ratification (CLAUDE.md 2). Stages D (continuous frontage), E (weapon-class reach 0.1/0.2/0.3 + pike troop type), F (historical revalidation + digest re-record), G (retire integer engine) remain.
source: audit/2026-07-22-mass-battle-stress-test/ (field-based stress test, spatial-model v2); Jordan in-session ruling 'why decelerate instead of collide / wouldn't that break charging'; analytic TOI verified vs scan+bisection oracle to ~1e-15 over 700 seeded pairs

system: mass_battle (tests/sim/mass_battle/geometry.py :: engaged_frontage + core/attrition.py :: _lanchester_strength + core/contact.py :: _find_contacts_standoff + orchestration.py exchange site)
description: Spatial-model v2 Stage D (the LAST live integer on the field position/contact path removed), per audit/2026-07-22-mass-battle-stress-test/spatial_model_v2_plan.md, Jordan-directed 'do frontage honestly'. Pre-v2 the melee Lanchester frontage term was len(set(int_col)) over the snapped engaged-cell recording -- the only integer left on the live contact path (backwards_analysis.md). Stage D replaces it with a CONTINUOUS front-overlap WIDTH derived from the OBB bodies. New geometry.engaged_frontage(a_boxes, b_boxes, heading): the union length, along a side's frontage axis (perpendicular to its facing), of each engaged cell body's width-interval clipped to the span the enemy bodies actually cover on that axis (pure float projection + interval merge; helpers _project_interval/_merge_intervals/_interval_union_length). _find_contacts_standoff now threads a_front/b_front (engaged frontage per side) onto each contact pair alongside the existing snapped a_cells/b_cells; _lanchester_strength gains a front_width arg that REPLACES the integer distinct-column count when supplied. SCOPING: the snapped (rank,file) cell identities are KEPT -- they key the formation-lattice casualty/density/stamina substrate (distribute_casualties, col_grid, _defender_depth, _fatigue_sigma), a discrete troop-block identity, NOT a live-position integer (I3's defensible-quantization carve-out); only the frontage MAGNITUDE moved to continuous. I4: the grid/OFF path pairs carry no *_front key -> orchestration passes p.get('a_front')=None -> _lanchester_strength falls back to the legacy integer count byte-exact (byte-exact grid oracle gate green, 30 passed). PROPERTIES verified (tests/valoria/test_frontage_conservation.py, 15 tests): reduces to len(set(int_col)) in the axis-aligned integer-lattice limit (each meeting file ~1.0); continuous/fractional for offset meetings (half-file offset -> 2.5, not 3); DEPTH-INVARIANT (depth-2 stacking does not inflate frontage -- union dedups files, so 'depth-2 no longer collapses'); FRONTAGE-CAPPED by the enemy's covered meeting span (Lanchester-linear guarantee, never square); strength LINEAR in front_width; I1 conservation holds across 5 seeded field battles; I2 determinism. [CALIBRATED-DEBT / DG-6 DISCLOSURE, not tuned] A/B (12-seed field battery, stash vs current): axis-aligned symmetric meetings (Line3v3, Column/heavy vs Line/levy, cav vs inf) are BYTE-IDENTICAL (the continuous width equals the integer count there); the shift appears only on offset/rotated/width-asymmetric meetings -- Arrowhead-vs-Line small, and Line4-vs-Line2 the wide attacker's overkill softens (A_win 12->10 of 12, defender hp-retained .452->.487) because its frontage is now correctly capped to the narrow defender's meeting width instead of counting extra integer columns. Lanchester melee exponent UNCHANGED (p=2.50 before and after -- the pre-existing DG-6 pool-variance artifact from ED-MB-0006/0007 is independent of frontage). This moves the DG-6-gated 20-row Cannae gauge (frontage-capped meetings now score continuously); the full gauge re-record + historical revalidation is Stage F (goldens deliberately NOT re-recorded pre-F per plan sec.7). No balance constant tuned. Adversarial pass: independent re-derivation of the frontage properties + interval-union edge cases (empty sides, depth dedup, wide-vs-narrow cap) before wiring; regression green (v2 acceptance 30, maneuvers/movement/yield 20 passed 1 xpassed pre-existing).
canonical_source: audit/2026-07-22-mass-battle-stress-test/spatial_model_v2_plan.md (sec.3 Stage D, sec.5, sec.9 P-DEC), backwards_analysis.md

description: Spatial-model v2 Stage E (weapon-class reach wired to the front face + author the pike troop type), per audit/2026-07-22-mass-battle-stress-test/spatial_model_v2_plan.md sec.3 Stage E and Jordan P-DEC-1 (sec.9). Replaces the flat REACH_SHORT=0.5 placeholder that Stages B/C carried (circle->OBB shape swap at unchanged reach) with the ruled per-troop-type FRONT-FACE reach in lattice units: non-pole melee 0.1 (levy/light_infantry/bare-'infantry'/unmapped default), pole/spear 0.2 (heavy_infantry), PIKE 0.3, cavalry/knights lance 0.2, ranged 0.1 melee sidearm (archers/crossbow/sling/artillery/mounted_archers; their projectile band stays VOLLEY_MAX_RANGE, reach-independent). reach_for now returns the mapped float (TROOP_TYPE_REACH), feeding cell_boxes_for -> geometry.obb_front_reach_overlap (contact, Stage B) + resolve_toi_and_commit (TOI halt, Stage C). Authored a `pike` troop type end-to-end: TROOP_TYPE_STATS['pike'] mirrors heavy_infantry EXACTLY (no fabricated stat line -- reach 0.3 is the sole differentiator; sec.B.2 carries no pike row, mirroring the closest canonical type is the non-inventing choice, flagged provisional-by-analogy pending an explicit sec.B.2 grounding), a `pike` weapon (pole/pierce/heavy, keywords pole+anti_cavalry) in equipment.weapons, a ('pike','medium') loadout, and defensive ShieldWall/Hold/Anvil roles. REACH ADVANTAGE -- MEASURED (the Stage E gate): it emerges through the ALREADY-WIRED charge-recoil reach gate PC_RECOIL_CHARGER_GATE (defender reach >= charger reach => the charge recoils). Before Stage E every troop shared reach 0.5 so the gate fired for everyone; with the differentiated values it is CONDITIONAL -- braced pike (0.3) / spear-heavy_infantry (0.2) repel a cavalry lance (0.2) and keep ~96.7% hp while the cavalry recoils to ~88.3%, whereas braced levy (0.1 < 0.2) is run down (defender ~90.7%, charge lands). The historically-correct anti-cavalry pike role, emergent from the reach data, no special case. DISCLOSED FINDING: reach differentiation does NOT change symmetric STANDING melee (pike-vs-levy standing == levy-vs-levy standing) -- the exchange is mutual once contact fires; reach only shifts contact TIMING. So reach is a charge/brace lever in this engine, not a standing-melee one; a directional-reach exchange term (longer weapon deals exclusive damage in the closing reach-gap window) is a possible future increment but carries a pike-pins-forever balance hazard under the halt-on-contact model and was NOT introduced here -- flagged for Jordan rather than unilaterally adding a potentially-unbalanced mechanic. I7 preserved: _effective_reach still gates reach to the forward FOV arc (facing-away cell gets 0). [DG-6 DISCLOSURE, not tuned] A/B (12-seed standing field battery, Stage D vs Stage E): symmetric matchups byte-identical, only the rotated Arrowhead-vs-Line shifts slightly (.9228/.9271 -> .9336/.9294) -- standing-melee gauge impact negligible; the decisive shift is charge/brace (measured above). NOTE flagged for Stage F: the 0.1/0.2/0.3 scale is finer than PP-290's Short<=1m (0.5)/Long<=3m (1.5) meter-grounding (REACH_SHORT/REACH_LONG kept defined+exported for PP-290 consumers) -- P-DEC-1 governs the field OBB model; the meter<->lattice reconciliation is deferred to Stage F's historical-revalidation pass, not silently overwritten. I4: reach_for reaches the grid path only via the 'kite'-instruction block (line ~1215), and the byte-exact battery (bat.py) fields no kite units, so the grid oracle is untouched (byte-exact grid test green). Verified: tests/valoria/test_reach_weapon_class.py (10). No balance constant tuned.

Spatial-model v2 Stage F (full verification + field-golden re-record + P-DEC-4 historical revalidation), per spatial_model_v2_plan.md sec.3 Stage F. No new mechanics -- re-runs every invariant gate over Stages D+E and re-records the field goldens. RESULTS (full detail in stage_F_verification.md): I1 conservation 0 violations / 300-battle field fuzz; I2 determinism (stress S5 PASS); I3 no integer on the live position/contact math path (only the formation-lattice cell-identity snap remains -- defensible quantization; the int(round) in _find_contacts_field is a non-live path); I4 byte-exact grid oracle green; I5 all shifts A/B'd + disclosed, no constant tuned; I6 mirror symmetry (stress S5 |skew|=0/48 exact); I7 facing-away -> 0 reach. Field goldens re-recorded (grid unchanged): unit_field 2da5183aeeedfadaf3acba1d72c2868b7b7d3a83a832df6e66d4f9d964f1bc38 (--check verified), cell_field 5f5db965f2712ae1b2b4fc614c827b6cb74a98fab4b4e9dfb410e5010ee5c273. Stress harness S0-S5 all green (S0 all 30 mechanics WIRED / none inert; S1 0 engine failures; S2 V-REFORM PASS; S3 all expected gates WIRED incl. the charge-recoil gates Stage E rides; S4 all off-by-default gates SAFE; S5 determinism+mirror PASS). Lanchester melee exponent p=2.50 unchanged. depth-2 experiment byte-identical before/after D+E (preserved, non-degenerate 89.5% retention). Full tests/valoria 447 passed / 60 skipped / 1 xpassed. P-DEC-4 HISTORICAL REVALIDATION (tests/sim/gauge_mb.py, history-grounded bands): pre-D baseline (A-C) = 10/20 multi; v2 (A-E) = 6/20 multi -- Stages D+E moved the gauge DOWN 4 rows. This is an authorized re-baseline (P-DEC-4 expected the frontage/reach change to move the gauge) but a material directional shift: the honest OBB geometry falls outside MORE current bands. Dominant failure class in BOTH baseline and v2 is DG-6 over-decisiveness (envelopment/force-ratio rows resolve 100%/0% vs historical bands). Confirmed mechanism: the melee pool sums N independent per-soldier dice, so its CV collapses ~1/sqrt(N) (measured 0.89->0.06 for N=4->1024) and the pool scales linearly with troops -> compute_degree near-deterministic from the force ratio -> certain, not banded, outcomes. Stage E win confirmed: C2/C6 (Cav vs BRACED Line) both REPEL, consistent with the historical repel bands. Per Jordan directive 2026-07-22 ('validate emergent results top-down from historical precedent; extend code as required to resolve standing issues using academic research, military theory, mathematics, historical precedent'), the DG-6 grounded resolution -- restoring scale-invariant outcome variance so a large advantage produces a decisive-but-uncertain (banded) result -- is UNDERWAY as a follow-on ED-MB (research grounding: stochastic Lanchester/breakpoint models, Clausewitz friction/du Picq morale, Sabin's Lost Battles decisiveness bands). Field goldens will re-record again when that resolution lands. No balance constant tuned in Stage F.

DG-6 over-decisiveness — GROUNDED partial resolution: per-battle combat-effectiveness (CEV) friction. Jordan directive 2026-07-22 ('validate emergent results top-down from historical precedent; extend code as required to resolve standing issues using academic research, military theory, mathematics, historical precedent'). Root cause CONFIRMED (mathematical): melee attrition sums N independent per-soldier dice -> CV self-averages ~1/sqrt(N) (measured 0.89->0.06 for N=4->1024); pool scales linearly with troops -> compute_degree near-deterministic from the force ratio -> outcomes collapse to 100%/0% where history shows bands. Fix (two research passes, ~200k tokens; grounding in audit/2026-07-22-mass-battle-stress-test/dg6_friction_resolution.md): keep the self-averaging attrition core, add a per-BATTLE, per-side multiplicative CEV factor M ~ LogNormal(0, sigma^2) drawn ONCE per battle (orchestration._draw_friction_cev, lazy so multi-turn re-entries keep the single draw), multiplying the subunit combat pool (core/exchange). Law of total variance: the attrition term ->0 with N (kept), the once-per-battle shock stays O(1) -> scale-invariant outcome variance (Dupuy CEV / Clausewitz friction / stochastic-Lanchester; Kingman 2002, Beyerchen 1992, Rowland, Sabin Lost Battles). CALIBRATED gauge-INDEPENDENTLY to the Dupuy DLEDB force-ratio win-curve (NOT the gauge, I5): sigma=1.1 reproduces 1.2:1->57% (t55), 1.5:1->62% (t62), 2:1->70% (t70), 3:1->82% (t80). Mechanism verified (test_friction_cev.py, 7): default-inert/byte-exact off; drawn once per battle not per turn; large-force 2:1 collapses to >=95% without friction and stays banded <90% with it (the scale-invariant-variance fix); I1/I2 hold. HONEST FINDING -- friction is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT, ships DEFAULT OFF: the 20-row history gauge moves 6/20 -> 4/20 with uniform friction ON, because (1) envelopment rows (H3/H4) stay ~100% -- their over-decisiveness is STRUCTURAL (envelopment geometry), needing a CONJUNCTIVE gate (Goldsworthy/Sabin: center-holds x cavalry-sufficiency x terrain), not symmetric noise; (2) friction BREAKS the Stage E brace repel (C2/C6 REPELLED->NOT-REPELLED: a big CEV draw lets cavalry break a braced line, but Waterloo-squares repel is near-deterministic) -- friction must be exempted/damped in the brace repel; (3) near-parity rows pushed just out of band. So it ships gated OFF (byte-exact, inert; grid oracle unaffected -- I4). Complete DG-6 resolution = (a) this CEV friction + (b) conjunctive envelopment gate + (c) brace-repel preservation -- scoped as follow-on. needs_jordan: default-flip + the (b)/(c) design calls. No constant tuned to the gauge.
